name: European plum (food medium)
description: Food-derived medium: metabolizable components of European plum (Prunus domestica) [Fruits] from FooDB, plus a standard M9 mineral base. Organic components use presence-based uptake bounds; FooDB content retained. Measured compounds with no BiGG exchange are listed under uncovered.

provenance.citation: FooDB v2020-04-07 (Wishart DS et al., foodb.ca). Content sources: DTU, DUKE, PHENOL EXPLORER, USDA.
provenance.notes: Organic components mapped from FooDB compounds to BiGG via InChIKey/name. Mineral base added. Bounds presence-based; foodb_content retained. Unmapped measured compounds kept in uncovered (v2 completeness fix). IMPORTANT: composition values are taken from the archived FooDB 2020-04-07 CSV data release; FooDB's live food page no longer displays this bulk composition, so it will appear empty. Verify individual compounds via the per-component cross-reference links. Underlying content was aggregated by FooDB from USDA / DTU / Phenol Explorer.
provenance.verification: expert-curated (multi-source food composition; growth-relevant profile completed, FooDB noise removed)
curation_sources: USDA FoodData Central FDC 169949 (Plums, raw; SR Legacy) - full macro/sugar/AA/mineral/vitamin/fatty-acid panel
Zhang et al. 2024, Food Chemistry: X, 'Soluble sugar, organic acid and phenolic composition and flavor evaluation of plum fruits', PMC11408021 - quantitative organic acids and sorbitol
Milosevic et al. 2022, chemical composition of European plum 'Cacanska Lepotica', PMC9498388 - organic-acid corroboration
provenance.curation_notes: Dominant carbon/energy sources are free glucose (5.1 g) > fructose (3.1 g) > sucrose (1.6 g), supplemented by sorbitol (~1.0 g) and malic acid (~0.8 g, the dominant organic acid). Principal organic-N source is aspartate/asparagine (0.35 g). Organic-acid and sorbitol values are cultivar-variable (ranges noted); all sugar/AA/mineral/vitamin/fatty-acid values from USDA FDC 169949.
curation_added: specific fermentable sugars with amounts (glucose 5.1, fructose 3.1, sucrose 1.6, galactose 0.14, maltose 0.08 g/100g)
D-sorbitol (defining fermentable polyol of Prunus, ~1.0 g/100g) - previously absent
organic acids with amounts: malic (dominant, 0.8 g), quinic (0.17 g), citric, tartaric, oxalic, succinic
complete amino acid profile with amounts (aspartate dominant at 0.35 g/100g)
full mineral panel with amounts (K 157 mg dominant)
full vitamin panel with amounts (C, B1/B2/B3/B5/B6, folate, choline, K, E)

curation_removed.name: Silver

curation_removed.reason: toxic metalloid contaminant, not a nutrient

curation_removed.reason: toxic heavy-metal contaminant, not a nutrient

curation_removed.reason: toxic heavy-metal contaminant, not a nutrient

curation_removed.name: Co2+
curation_removed.reason: cobalt is ultratrace in plum flesh and not a measured nutrient; FooDB noise

curation_removed.name: Nickel
curation_removed.reason: ultratrace contaminant element, not a defined plum nutrient

curation_removed.name: Chromium
curation_removed.reason: ultratrace, not a quantified plum nutrient

curation_removed.name: Molybdate
curation_removed.reason: ultratrace, not quantified in plum; drop as noise

curation_removed.name: Iodine c
curation_removed.reason: plums contain negligible/undetectable iodine; FooDB noise

curation_removed.name: Starch C12H20O10
curation_removed.reason: ripe plum starch is ~0 g/100g (USDA); removed as effectively absent

curation_removed.name: Iron (Fe3+)
curation_removed.reason: duplicate compartment-tagged iron entry; consolidated into single Iron

curation_removed.name: Fe2+ mitochondria
curation_removed.reason: duplicate compartment-tagged iron entry; consolidated into single Iron

curation_removed.name: Boron

curation_removed.name: Phlorizin
curation_removed.reason: apple-characteristic dihydrochalcone; FooDB cross-contamination, not a plum constituent

curation_removed.name: epsilon-Polylysine
curation_removed.reason: microbial fermentation product / food preservative additive, not a natural plum compound

curation_removed.name: (±)-erythro-Isoleucine
curation_removed.reason: predicted stereoisomer artifact, not a measured metabolite

curation_removed.name: (R)-Oxypeucedanin
curation_removed.reason: furanocoumarin typical of Apiaceae/citrus; implausible in plum flesh, likely FooDB predicted

components.name: D-Sorbitol
components.evidence_source: Zhang et al. 2024 Food Chem X, PMC11408021 (sorbitol mean 15.07% of total sugar, 0.86-29.81 mg/g FW)

components.name: L-Malate
components.evidence_source: Zhang et al. 2024 PMC11408021 (malic 177.6-1568.4 mg/100g, mean 73.7% of total acids)

components.name: Quinic acid
components.evidence_source: Zhang et al. 2024 PMC11408021 (quinic 11.0-420.3 mg/100g, mean 16.44% of total acids)

basis: per 100 g fresh weight (raw ripe Prunus domestica); concentration_mM assumes fruit density ~1 g/mL (100 g ~ 100 mL)
